"We’ve seen the growing online speculation, so while this is still very new, we wanted to provide some clarity,” read the statement, which both Batula and Wilson shared on social media.

“It was never our intention to purposely hide anything. Given the complicated relationship dynamics involved and the scrutiny that comes with being on a reality show, we needed a little space to process things privately before speaking on it,” they continued.
The couple went on to say their “connection” grew out of a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”
Miller recently posed a pointed question about hooking up with a friend's ex-boyfriend while interviewing Sydney Sweeney at the “Euphoria” Season 3 premiere on April 7.
“I mean, if you steal your friend’s ex-boyfriend ...” Miller asked Sweeney on the red carpet.
“It’s not good,” Sweeney said.

(Granted, Sweeney is involved in a a similar storyline on “Euphoria,” which returns to HBO Max on April 12. Sweeney’s character, Cassie, secretly hooked up with Nate (Jacob Elordi), the ex of her best friend Maddy (Alexa Demie), in Season 2 of the drama.)

At the "Euphoria" premiere, Miller went on to ask Sweeney, “What do you think is the next step? I feel like you ought to get married,” seemingly referencing the Season 3 trailer for “Euphoria," which shows Cassie walking down the aisle.
“I mean, I would hope so. You got to make it worth it,” Sweeney responded.
“You got to make it worth it,” Miller said, before turning and looking directly at the camera.
Cooke also addressed his ex Batula's new romance with Wilson during an April 1 interview for the “So Bad It’s Good With Ryan Bailey” podcast.
When host Ryan Bailey asked Cooke how he was doing, the reality star responded, “I’m good. You know, I appreciate people checking in. I’m actually really worried about Amanda if I’m being honest.”
Cooke said he'd spoken with Batula, whom he said had "mental health issues," on the nights before and after she and Wilson released their statements confirming their romance.
"I understand people have all sorts of opinions, and I’m not justifying any behavior. But like, from what I’m seeing, she’s kind of getting cyber bullied,” Cooke said.

Cooke went on to say Wilson "seems to be the kind of guy playing multiple women at the same time." He added Wilson had reached out to him in  “some half-a---ed text message" that he compared to a "word salad."
Cooke added that he was now questioning the timeline of Batula and Wilson's romance.

“It definitely makes me second guess a lot of things last summer, at a minimum," he said. "Just how checked out she was. In my own unsuccessful ways, I was still trying to put an effort in. I mean, it’s a mental mind f--- for me.”
"I’m still trying to figure out the details. But I know I can imagine West is using the same charm that he’s used on just about every other chick," he added.

title: 'Laguna Beach' Cast Now: What Happened After the MTV Show Ended?
summary: Where is the Laguna Beach cast now? They're reuniting for a reunion on April 10. Read on for an update about their lives now.
story: Nearly 20 years after "Laguna Beach" aired its last episode, the cast of the MTV reality series is getting back together for a reunion.
On April 10, "The Reunion: Laguna Beach" will air on the Roku Channel.

Several members of the original cast will appear in the reunion special, including Lauren Conrad, Stephen Colletti, Kristin Cavallari, Trey Phillips, Talan Torriero, Christina Schuller, Lo Bosworth, Dieter Schmitz, Jessica Smith, Alex Hooser and Morgan Olsen.
Before the reunion premieres, we're sharing an update on what some of the cast members have been up to over the last two decades.
Kristin Cavallari
"Laguna Beach" viewers were always Team Lauren or Team Kristin in the Kristin-Lauren-Stephen love triangle.
In 2022, Kristin Cavallari and Stephen Colletti opened up about their enduring friendship in an episode of their “Laguna Beach” rewatch podcast, “Back to the Beach with Kristin and Stephen.”
“We were great. I mean, I would say you were my first love. Like, we really had a very sweet, great relationship,” Cavallari said.
In the episode, Cavallari revealed that she and Colletti have "stayed in touch over the years" and said they "reconnected a little bit more in the last year and a half."

After "Laguna Beach" ended, Cavallari appeared on 24 episodes of the show's spinoff series, "The Hills," per IMDB. Later, she also starred in an E! reality series called “Very Cavallari” and a docuseries called "Honestly Cavallari: The Headline Tour."

Cavallari has made guest appearances in several scripted TV series over the years, including "9-1-1:Nashville," "The League," "The Middle" and "CSI: NY." She also competed in Season 13 of "Dancing With the Stars."
In 2016, Cavallari published a book titled, "Balancing in Heels: My Journey to Health, Happiness, and Making It All Work." She's also the author of three cookbooks.
Cavallari married football player Jay Cutler in 2013. The couple went on to have three children. In 2020, the couple announced that they were getting a divorce.
These days, Cavallari keeps busy with her podcast "Let’s Be Honest with Kristin Cavallari."
In 2025, Cavallari appeared at the Shop TODAY Stages event and spoke about her work as an entrepreneur and her successful lifestyle brand, Uncommon James.
Lauren Conrad

"Laguna Beach" may have pit Lauren Conrad and Kristin Cavallari against each other, but the former rivals cleared the air in 2022 during an episode of the “Back to the Beach with Kristin and Stephen” podcast.

“My take on it is, you and I really never had any beef, really” Cavallari told Conrad. “Obviously, there was a little truth to what happened with the three of us. I felt like MTV coming kept it alive and made it way worse than it ever would have been.”
“Yeah, I think it was done when they started filming,” Conrad said.

Later on, Cavallari said they "never had an issue" with each other, but Conrad said, “I mean, we had an issue with each other. But it had been squashed. They ran with it. It made for an interesting show.”
They spoke more about their feud in an interview with TODAY.com ahead of the premiere.
“Obviously, it’s been so long. I mean, I’m just happy that we’ve gotten this time together where we can truly just put everything in the past. It’s been really nice,” Cavallari said.

After the end of "Laguna Beach," Conrad starred in the spinoff series, "The Hills," which followed her as she attended FIT and pursued a career in the fashion industry. The show also highlighted her internships with Teen Vogue and People's Revolution.

Today, Conrad is the designer behind the womenswear line LC Lauren Conrad and the children's line Little Co. In 2013, Conrad and her friend Hannah Skvarla founded a nonprofit online fair trade shop called The Little Market.
In 2014, Conrad married former musician, William Tell, who was part of the band Something Corporate and now works as a lawyer. The couple went on to welcome two sons.
Lo Bosworth

During her "Laguna Beach" days, Lo Bosworth was pretty tight with Lauren Conrad. In 2024, Bosworth told E! News she was still in touch with her high school pal but said she's “not in constant touch with a ton of people from the show.”

In a recent post on her Instagram page, Bosworth reflected on her time on the reality series, writing, "I was 16, had no idea what I was doing, and somehow that became the start of a semi public life that I’ve been navigating ever since."
After wrapping up "Laguna Beach," Bosworth appeared in the show's spinoff series, "The Hills."

Bosworth attended the University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA) then moved to New York City, where she started a cooking and lifestyle blog. In a recent Instagram post, she explained that she had "severe vitamin deficiencies and was seeking answers" at the time.
Bosworth later created Love Wellness, a women's wellness brand that's focused on vaginal health, hormones, gut health and mental health.
"Took about 10 years, a lot of tears, and more product development meetings than I can count. I love my job!!!" she wrote on Instagram.
In July 2025, Bosworth married businessman Dom Natale. The couple welcomed their first child together, a daughter named Nelle, in January 2026.
Bosworth currently has a podcast called "Tell Me I'm a Good Mom" and a Substack newsletter about wellness.
Stephen Colletti
Stephen Colletti was known as the heartthrob of the group and was involved in a love triangle with Conrad and Cavallari during his "Laguna Beach" years.
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Colletti reflected on some of his romantic escapades from his younger years.
“I made some decisions back in the day that I’m not proud of and and we’ve had a lot of growth since then,” he said.
Overall, Colletti said the show’s producers “took their liberties” and dramatized the love triangle aspect of the show.

"When it comes to the love triangle, from the jump, they made it seem like Lauren and I were just straight up having this affair out in the open when I never cheated on Kristen during our relationship. Lauren and I never did anything like that," he said.

Following his time on "Laguna Beach," Colletti starred as Chase in "One Tree Hill" and appeared in 57 episodes. Since then, he's built up several other acting credits in projects like the TV series "Everyone Is Doing Great," the TV movie "Hometown Christmas" and several others.
Colletti also appeared in Season 4 of the Peacock reality series "Traitors."
In his personal life, Colletti is currently expecting his first child with his wife, NASCAR host and reporter Alex Weaver. The couple got married in October 2025.
Morgan Smith
After "Laguna Beach," Morgan Smith (formerly Olsen) pursued a career in the fashion industry like several of her former co-stars.
Smith, who now lives in South Carolina with her husband and three children, has a clothing brand named minnow that makes clothes for women, men and children.
The brand's website says minnow is "a reflection of the life and style of its founder, Morgan Smith, a mother and entrepreneur with a background in premium product marketing."
Per the website, Smith previously worked in both Los Angeles and New York.
Christina Sinclair
Christina Sinclair (formerly Schuller) spoke to Time in 2014 to discuss the experience of signing up for "Laguna Beach."

"We’re sitting there in class, and over the daily announcements they announce, ‘OK everyone, MTV’s here. They’re going to cast a reality show on Laguna Beach. If you’re interested, sign up in the quad at break.’ And I think for any 18 year old, that’s a dream come true," she said.
After "Laguna Beach," she attended the University of Southern California and moved back to her hometown to teach fitness classes.
Per Sinclair’s website, she works as a certified fitness instructor who specializes in barre fitness. In 2012, she was ranked a top 20 barre instructor in Los Angeles.
Sinclair is married to her husband, Chad, and the couple has three children.
Jason Wahler
Jason Wahler, who was known for his rocky relationship with Conrad, didn't have a stellar reputation when he appeared on "Laguna Beach" and "The Hills."
While struggling with substance abuse, Wahler appeared on “Celebrity Rehab with Dr. Drew” in 2010.
In 2016, Wahler opened up to Entertainment Tonight about his addiction journey, noting that he particularly struggled with alcohol and cocaine.
"I became a totally different person active in my addiction versus when I’m sober and living life in recovery," he said.
Wahler was arrested several times and sought treatment at multiple rehab centers over the years. He told Entertainment Tonight he was inspired to really change his life after attempting suicide.
"My addiction drove me to suicide," he said. "Not contemplation -- actually attempting suicide -- and somebody found me and that’s why I’m still here today. I’m very grateful for that."
Wahler married his girlfriend Ashley Slack in 2013. The couple now has three children together.
Since starting his recovery journey, Wahler has been transparent about the highs and lows he's encountered over time, including a relapse in 2018.

Wahler, who appeared on "The Hills: New Beginnings," describes himself as a "passionate advocate for addiction education and prevention" on his website. He works as a guest speaker and recovery advocate.
Wahler is currently the director of the Change Your Brain Foundation and is on the board of Cure Addiction Now.
Trey Phillips
Along with several of his classmates, Trey Phillips appeared on "Laguna Beach" as a high school student.
When Phillips was a guest on Colletti and Cavallari's podcast in 2022, fans of "Laguna Beach" learned that he's kept in touch with Colletti since filming wrapped many years ago.
Cavallari also complimented Phillips on how put together he was while they were starring in the show, saying, "You were so mature and so wise beyond your years."

After high school, Phillips attended Bard College to study human rights. He later dropped out to pursue a career in fashion and tried to create his own hat and sunglasses companies. When those failed, he went on to study fashion and later scored a job as a designer for Vera Wang, a company he worked at for seven years.
Later, Phillips relocated to London to continue his fashion career.
Talan Torriero
In "Laguna Beach," Talan Torriero was known as a bit of a Casanova and was romantically linked to Cavallari at times. But he told E! News in 2022 that he was really a "hopeless romantic."
"I loved being in relationships," he said.
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Torriero confirmed that he dated singer Nicole Scherzinger in the early 2000s.
"She was a big part of my life. I’m really happy to see all the success she’s had," he said.
Torriero got married to his wife Danielle in 2014. The couple shares three children.

"I think that young Talon had a good time. He had a lot of fun. Talon today is boring. He’s married. He’s got three kids. He’s got basketball practice on Saturday, so he’s got to get home tomorrow," he recently told Entertainment Tonight.
Dieter Schmitz
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Schmitz described what made the reality show so popular, saying the group had been friends since the first grade.
“I think that’s what made the show special,” he said.
Looking back on his experience of filming a reality show, Schmitz joked that he has one regret.
"I said a lot of bad words and they didn't use beeps, so I would just go quiet. And my mom was like, 'Your mic always goes out,'" he teased.
Life has changed a lot for Dieter Schmitz since his "Laguna Beach" days. On his Instagram page, the former reality star describes himself as a "proud papa of two," a "husband" and a "hotelier."
When Schmitz got married in 2016, Colletti served as a groomsman.
During his interview with Entertainment Tonight, Schmitz said he typically doesn't bring up his time on reality TV at work, but noted that the reunion had "brought it back a little bit."
"All my coworkers that I hadn’t told, they’re like, 'What is happening?'" he said.
Jessica Evans

Jessica Evans (formerly Smith) appears in the "Laguna Beach" reunion special. During an interview with Entertainment Tonight, the former reality star said she still keeps in touch with Cavallari, Torriero and Olsen.
“We’ve got our little fab four group chat that we text on all the time so that’s great. We’re all very close. We were all in the same graduating class as well,” she said.
Evans, who has been married almost 17 years, said her husband hasn't watched "Laguna Beach."
“He’s just not a reality TV guy,” she said.
However, a few of her four children may be interested in seeing the show.

"I told my kids that they could watch it when they are the age that I was when I was on it. So my oldest is 15, which is how old I was. He has no interest. My 13-year-old, who is here with me right now, enlightens me today that she’s watched Season 1 at the neighbor’s house. I was like, 'Great. Well, better than Season 2, right?' I was like, 'Don’t go into Season 2 yet. Wait for me for that,'" she said.
Alex Hooser

Fans of "Laguna Beach" will recall an Alex Hooser appearing on the show. Now, she goes by Lauren Olsen (her married name). Her birth name was Lauren Alexandria Hooser, as she explained in a recent Instagram video.
"I've always been Lauren. Up until about third grade is when I changed to being Alex or Alexandria, my middle name," she said.
As it turns out, her family moved to Laguna Beach during the summer of third grade and a young Lauren decided it was time for a change.
"As we're registering for school, I asked my parents if I could go by my middle name, Alexandria," she said.
Olsen captioned her post, "Lauren, Alex, Lauren Olsen, call me whatever you want at this point 😂."
In a recent interview with Entertainment Tonight, Olsen also addressed her name change.
"My real name is Lauren, which I kind of like to go back to. I feel like Hannah Montana. I can be like, 'This is my Hannah.' And then back home, I’m Lauren," she said.
Olsen was always close to Cavallari during filming, and she said they have "maintained" their friendship.

"Kristen is the real deal when it comes to a best friend. And I’m forever grateful for that friendship. And even though we haven’t necessarily seen each other throughout the years, we’ve been pretty good at staying in touch. And I love that girl, man. She’s ride or die till the end. And so am I," she said.
After "Laguna Beach" ended, Olsen lived in Los Angeles for a while before moving back to Laguna Beach.

Olsen is now married and has two children. She and her husband lived in Hawaii for around 12 years before relocating to California to be closer to family, as she revealed in an episode of Cavallari and Colletti's podcast in 2022.

title: Is it Really Possible to Watch Coachella at Home? Yes — Here’s How
summary: Here's how you can stream the performances at Coachella.
story: If you can’t make it to Coachella, don’t worry — Coachella can come to you.

The 25th edition of the famed Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival, which takes place at the Empire Polo Club in Indio, California, kicks off April 10. A wide range of stars will perform, including Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Teddy Swims and Karol G.
The event runs over two weekends. The first is April 10, 11 and 12, followed by April 17, 18 and 19.

This year’s event will also be available to watch at home, so if you don’t plan to trek to the desert, you can slip into your jammies, fire up your computer or phone and still take in what the festival’s musical acts have to offer.
How to Watch Coachella At Home For Free
The festival’s performances will be available to stream on YouTube.
Head to Coachella’s page and scroll to the stage you’d like to watch, or click here for a playlist of all the available livestreams, which are set to start at 4 p.m. PT on April 10.
In addition to the main stage, or the Coachella Stage, shows will be held on seven other stages: Outdoor Theatre, Sahara, Mojave, Gobi, Sonora and Quasar.
Here’s the Full Coachella Schedule
There are some real heavyweights who will take the main Coachella stage. (All times are PT.)
Sabrina Carpenter will perform from 9:05 p.m. until 10:35 p.m. on April 10. She will be followed at midnight by electronic artist Anyma. Teddy Swims will perform earlier in the night at 5:30 p.m.
Bieber will headline on April 11 with an 11:25 p.m. show. That will wrap up a night that features performances from other acts such as The Strokes at 9 p.m. and Addison Rae at 5:30 p.m.
The final night on April 12 will culminate with Karol G’s 9:55 p.m. set. Young Thug will perform before her at 7:50 p.m.
The whole livestream schedule is available here.

You can check the entire Coachella schedule for a list of all the talent performing, as well as what stage they’ll be on and what time their sets begin. More highlights include Katseye at 8 p.m. Friday on the Sahara stage, Sombr at 7:05 p.m. Saturday at the Outdoor Theatre, FKA Twigs at 8:45 p.m. Sunday on the Mojave stage and more.
The schedule for the second weekend is not yet available.

title: The 4 Books Harlan Coben Is Reading and Loving Lately
summary: Bestselling author Harlan Coben stopped by TODAY on April 10 to share the books he's reading and recommending this month
story: Bestselling author Harlan Coben stopped by TODAY on April 10 to share the books he's reading and recommending this month.

The author also has exciting news: He's writing a memoir! "Plot Twist: Life, Craft, and the Messy First Draft," out in September, tracks Coben's life as a prolific writer. Coben has written 35 books and counting and has sold over 100 million copies.
"I wrote a how-to book. I want to inspire you to write, create or paint. All the things I've learned, I'm passing them along," he said on TODAY.
Below, he shares the books he's reading and loving lately.
Best Mystery
Mad Mabel by Sally Hepworth
Mad Mabel is a funny, inspiring, story of an underestimated woman about to prove everyone wrong.
At 15, Elsie Fitzpatrick became Australia's youngest convicted murderer. Now 81, Elsie has left that behind her — until a young girl moves in across the road and unearths her past.
"Sally Hepworth is a huge star in Australia. This is the book that's going to make her a huge star here," Coben said on TODAY. He calls this book "funny, twisty and suspenseful."
Best Contemporary Read
The Left And The Lucky by Willy Vlautin
This text is an honest display of the power of friendship and how, in various ways, it can save our lives.

"The Left and the Lucky" is the latest from an author who is known for his portraits of contemporary American life. In this one, a young boy befriends his next-door neighbor, offering him an escape from his tumultuous family life.
Coben calls this book "gut-wrenching" and "compassionate," with shades of John Steinbeck. There's "dignity and integrity" in this working-class story, he says.
Best Family Drama
This Is Not About Us by Allegra Goodman
Filled with humor, this is a story about growing old and the love that connects a family across generations.

How is it possible that an apple pie could cause a years-long rift between sisters? After Sylvia and Helen Rubinstein's sister passes away, their relationship is in turmoil, enough so that a baked good could cause a major problem.
Coben calls this his "favorite novel of the year" so far.
What Harlan's Reading
Ghost Town by Tom Perrotta
Ghost Town illuminates how the past haunts the present, even when we think we’ve left it behind.
For his 11th novel, Tom Perrotta returned to the New Jersey of his youth to write a story of a teenager whose life spurs into a different, and somewhat supernatural, direction after extreme grief.
"You'll love every page," Coben said.

title: Noah Kahan Shares the Advice From His Therapist He's 'Never Been Able to Do'
summary: Noah Kahan revealed that he fears he's already peaked in life during a candid conversation from his documentary "Out of Body," which premieres on Netflix April 13
story: Noah Kahan revealed that he fears he's already peaked in life during a candid conversation from his documentary "Out of Body," which premieres on Netflix April 13.

Kahan, the singer songwriter who skyrocketed to fame with the 2022 album "Stick Season," opened up about his complicated feelings surrounding his hometown, his success and his mental health in a new clip from the documentary, which aired exclusively on TODAY April 10.
"When I was in Hanover, Strafford, I felt suffocated by it," Kahan said. "And then when I left, I yearned for like, its simplicity."
"You can't just escape who you are because you moved to a different place," he added.
Speaking with his brother while looking at the New York City skyline, Kahan was asked if he worries that he's peaked or will peak.
"Uh yeah, yes I do," he answered. "All the time. That's all I think about, really."

"You know, tomorrow if I don't play a great show at MSG like, what could that mean? That could mean that people (start) losing interest, and the fans start to think it's boring and stale, and the label loses faith in me."
He then summarized advice he's received from a therapist: "Failure is part of it, and you need to prepare yourself for that, because it'll let you accept the moment you're in."
"I just have never really been able to do that," he added.

Kahan debuted on the music season with the 2019 album "Busyhead." He went on to release an EP "Cape Elizabeth" in 2020 followed by "I Was / I Am" in 2021. But it was "Stick Season," which features hits like "Northern Attitude," "Homesick" and "Dial Drunk," that made him a household name.
Kahan is set to release his first follow-up to that album, "The Great Divide," on April 24, and he's set to go back out on tour in June.
The upcoming Netflix doc will feature footage from a year and a half in Kahan's life, he revealed in an Instagram post.

"It was weird, difficult, and genuinely beautiful to experience," he wrote. "This documentary captures so much more than I ever thought the world would see about myself, my creative journey and my family. I am nervous about it because it’s personal and some things I touch on are painful to think about and be reminded of. I remember though that anything scary or hard is usually worth sharing with the world."

title: TODAY Makeover Gives 1 Mom a Dramatic Hair Refresh: ‘I Feel Like an ‘It’ Girl’
summary: In a TODAY Show makeover segment with Jenna Lyons and Chris Appleton, one mom gets a hair transformation with a platinum blond bob.
story: One New York mom of two is feeling like an “It” girl after her Pop-Up Makeover on TODAY.

On the April 9 episode of TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle, the two co-hosts were joined by celebrity hairstylist Chris Appleton and fashion businesswoman Jenna Lyons, who introduced their latest makeover nominee.

For a Pop-Up Makeover, Appleton and Lyons surprise one deserving viewer with a hair and style transformation. In this segment, Appleton and Lyons visited an unsuspecting mom named Courtney in Astoria, New York, and revealed that her sister-in-law Tanjila nominated her for a makeover.
Tanjila wrote to the duo that she wanted Courtney, a 39-year-old administrator at New York University, to experience the transformation because she “prioritizes everyone but herself.”

Courtney spent the last 10 years focusing on her career and marriage while raising her two children and renovating her home. Last year, she also decided to start her fitness journey. So, she was in need of an updated look and wardrobe to match the new her.
When Appleton and Lyons found Courtney and Tanjila in New York, the administrator was shocked to see them.

“I’m a mom, so I haven’t had time to get my hair done and really have a day where I feel confident and beautiful,” Courtney told Appleton and Lyons. “I’m so excited! I can’t believe this is happening. This is crazy!”
Appleton and Lyons then whisked Courtney away for her day of full glam.
Tanjila and Courtney’s best friend Maria joined Jenna Bush Hager, Sheinelle Jones, Lyons and Appleton in the studio for the mom’s grand reveal.

Courtney strutted out, debuting her platinum blond bob. She had a pep in her step as she twirled to model her Aritzia crescendo sweater, Aritzia Henrietta midi black satin skirt with white lace trim and a Sam Edelman oversized black leather clutch. A pair of Sam Edelman black strappy kitten heels completed her look.
“Oh my God, you look amazing!” Tanjila told her sister-in-law.
Maria chimed in and said, “My hands are shaking! Wow, you look so beautiful.”
Courtney was also a big fan of her makeover.
“I love it. I feel so confident, so beautiful. I feel like an ‘It’ girl in New York City, and I always felt like that on the inside,” she said. “It was amazing. Thank you guys so much.”

Jenna applauded her look, particularly her shorter, lighter do. Appleton mentioned that the style is trending right now thanks to Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy, adding that Courtney’s cut is a “quiet luxury bob.”
He also said the cut has versatility, allowing Courtney to wear it straight or wavy, and that the color makes her blue eyes pop.
“Out with the old and in with the new, and she fully embraced it,” Appleton said.
As for her styling, Lyons said she appreciated that Courtney was open to try something new.
She recalled Courtney saying after seeing the clothes picked out for her, “‘I would never have pulled any of this. Let’s try it all on.’”
Lyons wanted to choose items that could be dressed up or down depending on the day.
Courtney said she was looking forward to wearing her outfit for a date night with her husband. At the end of the segment, she hugged her sister-in-law and friend and thanked them again.

title: 'Who's the Boss?' Star Danny Pintauro Is Driving for Amazon: 'There's No Shame'
summary: Danny Pintauro reveals he is driving for Amazon Flex while reviving his acting career, opening up about Hollywood's struggles, side hustles and staying hopeful for the future.
story: Former child star Danny Pintauro, best known for playing Jonathan Bower on the hit sitcom “Who’s the Boss?” recently revealed that he has been driving for Amazon Flex as he works to navigate a difficult entertainment industry and revive his acting career.
“Working hard while ‘not working,’” Pintauro, 50, wrote on Instagram. “38 packages today!”
“There’s no shame in staying in motion,” he added.

Pintauro, who appeared on all eight seasons of the beloved ABC sitcom alongside Tony Danza, Judith Light and Alyssa Milano, opened up to TODAY.com about the realities of trying to make it in Hollywood decades after his child-star fame.
Though posting about the job felt vulnerable, Pintauro says he never felt ashamed.
“The business has changed so dramatically,” he says.

Pintauro described pilot season in the 1980s and ‘90s as “nuts,” recalling a time when roughly 100 pilots were produced each year and aspiring actors flocked to Los Angeles hoping to land roles. Now, he says, opportunities have dwindled so sharply that one network recently ordered just eight pilots, a sign, he says, of how much the industry has shrunk for actors trying to find work today.
Los Angeles, he says and Film LA reported, had roughly 18,560 shoot days in 2021; by 2024, that number had fallen to just 7,716.

For Pintauro, driving for Amazon Flex is simply practical. He says the job’s flexible schedule allows him to keep auditioning while earning money, and gives him a level of privacy that more public-facing work would not.

“I take a picture and walk away. No one sees me,” he says, noting that the most common response he hears online is from fans joking that they would be thrilled if he showed up at their door with a delivery.
By sharing his experience publicly, Pintauro says he also hopes to break the stigma around actors taking regular jobs between roles.
“Honest work is not embarrassing,” he says.
Many other actors are also taking on outside work — including driving for Amazon Flex — though Pintauro declined to name names.
“We’re all pivoting.”

Pintauro says that many people misunderstand the finances of being a former television star, noting that residuals from a sitcom that aired in the 1980s are not enough to live on. Instead, he says he has spent recent years juggling multiple jobs while trying to rebuild his acting career, including teaching, running his acting coaching business, “The Resonant Actor,” and building decorative book nooks.

Pintauro says he remains hopeful that his next chapter in Hollywood is still ahead. He acknowledges that returning to the industry has come with added challenges, including being openly gay, living with HIV, and still carrying the public perception of Jonathan Bower decades after “Who’s the Boss?” ended. But despite the obstacles, he says, he remains confident that the right opportunity will come.
“A surprising number of people are like, ‘Have patience. I can feel that there is something coming for you,’” Pintauro tells TODAY.com. “So I just have to be patient.”

title: Patrick Ball Says He Almost Appeared in SNL's 'Pitt' Sketch. He Watched From Lorne Michaels' Office Instead — With Isa Briones
summary: Patrick Ball talks about watching The Pitt SNL sketch from Lorne Michaels' office, meeting Harry Potter and why he stopped engaging with fans' tweets.
story: Patrick Ball doesn't consider himself a "pop culture consumer." But with his starring role as Dr. Frank Langdon on "The Pitt," he's found himself at the center of one of its hottest shows — and meeting Harry Styles because of it.

In the March 15 episode of "SNL," Harry Styles pulled double duty and host and musical guest. In one sketch, titled "MAHAspital," Styles stepped into a spoofed version of "The Pitt," which follows hours in the life of emergency room doctors at the fictional Pittsburgh Trauma Medical Center.

In a twist, the skit sees the doctors in the Pitt treat medical ailments and several trauma not with traditional medicine, but with things associated with pseudoscience and Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s "Make America Healthy Again" movement.
Ball tells TODAY.com he was asked about 24 hours before air to be a part of the skit.
When it didn't work out, he and his co-star Isa Briones — who are both currently starring on Broadway — watched the episode from 30 Rock in none other than Lorne Michaels' office.

"Me and Isa got to go and we got to see Lorne’s office with all the little index cards and the run down and and watch the show," Ball says. "And then went to the after party, and we got to meet Harry Styles, and I watched Isa absolutely lose her freaking cool. "

"The Pitt" premiered on HBO Max in January 2025 and is currently airing its second season. Ball's character dramatically exited the first season after senior attending resident Dr. Michael "Robby" Robinavitch (Noah Wyle) found out he had been stealing drugs from the emergency department.
Season 2, which is set on the Fourth of July, marks Langdon's first shift back after going to rehab, reuniting him with Robby, Santos and, to the delight of fans, Dr. Mel King (Taylor Dearden).
In an interview with TODAY.com Ball discusses what we didn't see in Langdon's personal life, his experience on Broadway in "Becky Shaw" and his reaction to Mel and Langdon, or "Kingdon," shippers.
This interview has been lightly edited for clarity.

I think one of the strengths of the show and the one day format that John (Wells) and (R. Scott Gemmill) have set up is that it leaves a lot of room to wonder for the audience, and a lot of room for people to do their own detective work and write their own narrative on top of it. Langdon's been away for 10 months. Part of that has been going to rehab and began the process of recovery. Another part of that, he has been out of the Pitt, and he has been away from his job. You meet Langdon in Season 1, who, there are little cracks that you see. Him feeling like he needs to buy a dog in order to make things right at home. You can sort of wonder what all of that's about. He has two kids at home. He has a wife that does the bulk of caregiving while he understands his place in the world as being the the wage earner.

And then that gets interrupted because he's not at work, and he's not earning and he's at home, playing a different role within the family for the first time. That I bet was a pretty complicated experience. And I think it's really a testament to John and Scott to not over explain that and leave room for people to wonder what that looks like.

Yeah, we learned from that scene with Dana (Katherine LaNasa) in midseason that I don't think he had the reach out and the support that he maybe anticipated. I think he's also had to re-evaluate just who he is within this community. I think in Season 1, you have a Langdon who's pretty sure that he is a leader and he is a quarterback for the team. And I think not hearing from his friends and co-workers for 10 months probably made him reassess just who he was in this community.

No, no. This is an old hat for her, but she's so good. Me and Elysia (Roorbach) went and watched her Sunday night, we went and saw her in her show, and she was just absolutely phenomenal. You can just tell that she's a theater creature. Like she just has that power, that soul power, and and it was just amazing seeing her, like, in her bag.

Yeah, it moves really fast. There is sort of a kinetic-ness that is fairly theatrical. But one thing you just only get on stage, and you only get in a place like Broadway, is you get an audience. You get to feel that energy back and forth and and that's just something that doesn't exist whenever you're shooting on the lot. And so I've just been having the absolute time of my life.

I've had to learn whenever this train first got going in Season 1, like it was really fun to sort of engage and try to stay accessible and stay part of the conversation, and then as things continue to snowball, I realized, "Oh this space is actually not for me." And a lot of fans get a lot of joy and, frankly, build a lot of community with one another through this process of shipping and writing their own narratives and creating their own theories. And that really doesn't have anything to do with me.

But I think part of the utility of this show, and I think maybe the main utility of this show, is to create conversation and create community. It's really awesome to see that community get so activated. But I've had to learn that is something that we are part of instigating, but that is a community that we actually, unfortunately, don't get to belong to, because as soon as we step into that space, it changes the space.

It was amazing. I actually got a call late, late, midnight, the night before. They were trying to get me in the skit, and then it just didn't — we didn't have time to go through the proper channels and get it cleared. So it didn't work out. But then they're like, "Hey, well, do you want to come and watch it from Lorne (Michael's) office?" And so me and Isa got to go and we got to see Lorne's office with all the little index cards and the run down and and watch the show. And then went to the after party, and we got to meet Harry Styles, and I watched Isa absolutely lose her freaking cool.
It was amazing. I have never seen an audience become so spellbound in my life. Like he walked in and people just like, went into a trance. It was unbelievable.

I mean, I'm not ... lowkey, I'm not the biggest pop culture consumer, and so, I wasn't 100% familiar with just how overwhelming that spell is, but then I saw it, and then I met him, and I was like, "Oh, wow, this is for real. I get it." This is an incredibly, overwhelmingly charming person.

title: Regé-Jean Page, Halle Bailey on the Higher Stakes of Romance Film ‘You, Me & Tuscany’
summary: Regé-Jean Page and Halle Bailey’s new movie, “You, Me & Tuscany,” features a Black love story, a rarity in today’s movie industry that the film’s stars are keenly aware of.
story: Regé-Jean Page and Halle Bailey’s new movie, “You, Me & Tuscany,” features a Black love story, a rarity in today’s movie industry that the film’s stars are keenly aware of.

The romantic comedy, out in theaters on April 10, follows Halle’s character, Anna, who decides to book a spontaneous trip to Tuscany after encountering an Italian man named Matteo (Lorenzo de Moor) who owns a villa there. When she arrives, she meets Matteo’s cousin Michael, Page’s character, and the two begin to fall for each other.

Leading up to its release, social media users have been encouraging fans to see the movie not only to experience Page and Bailey’s palpable chemistry on the big screen, but also because of reportedly bigger stakes hinging on the film’s success. Even award-winning filmmaker Nina Lee said on X that she was told by a studio and executive that they are waiting to see how well “You, Me & Tuscany” does at the box office before approving other Black-led projects.
When Page and Bailey appeared on TODAY April 7, Craig Melvin asked the two about the wider conversation surrounding their film.

“Everyone has their favorite rom-com, but the reality is that they’re not a lot of rom-coms out there where they feature folks that look like us,” Craig noted. “I read recently that there are even some studios that are actively watching to see how this movie does before they greenlight other rom-coms featuring people that look like us.”
Craig asked, “What kind of message do you hope this film sends?”
Page replied that he wants audiences to see how relatable the film is and that anyone can connect to the movie’s themes.

“I love that everyone who walks out that cinema feels like this is for them, the aspirational, whimsical, joyful escapades into the sunset, finding your perfect life, finding your perfect love, everyone can have that. Everyone can do that,” he said. “We didn’t make Tuscany up. It’s not a fantasy movie. It’s fantastical.”
He hopes that moviegoers leave theaters feeling inspired by the characters to take a chance and pursue their passions.
“Absolutely everyone deserves that and everyone does live that in real life,” he added.
Bailey agreed and pointed out the importance of making a Black-led romance.

She said, “We need to see more of our stories on screen. I mean, it’s beautiful that we have a theatrical release. People can go to the theaters to see this movie and experience this joy and adventure, and go for this wild ride.”
Add TODAY to Google
Add TODAY to Google
“You, Me & Tuscany” producer Will Packer, the filmmaker behind hits like “Girls Trip” and “Think Like a Man,” also stopped by TODAY to chat about the movie.
During his interview with Craig, Al Roker and Laura Jarrett on April 8, he spoke about representation on the big screen, too.
Packer said, “First of all, (it’s a) romantic comedy. So, Hollywood is not making a lot of romantic comedies for theaters, right? I think this is a great time, because we need that escapism.”
He said sitting in a theater with strangers and having a good time creates a communal environment.

The producer then continued, “They already don’t make a lot of films with Black leads. So the fact that we have a movie that happens to have Black leads in the theaters — lot of eyeballs to see how this one does.”
Packer previously responded to Lee’s thread on X about his film and its impact on the movie industry.
He wrote, in part, that “You, Me & Tuscany”’s success “could change a lot of people’s lives.”

title: 'Temptation Island' Has 4 New Couples, Including 1 Struggling to Move Past Infidelity: 'Scar Never Goes Away'
summary: Who are the "Temptation Island" Season 2 couples? See a cast guide and later see the temptors who are joining the show.
story: "Temptation Island" is returning to Netflix for another season.
The reality dating series, which originally debuted in 2001, previously aired on both Fox and USA Network before finding a new home on Netflix in 2025.
The latest season of "Temptation Island," which Netflix is referring to as Season 2, will hit Netflix on April 10, with all episodes premiering at once.

Similar to last season, four couples will put their relationships to the test and mingle with a group of singles to see if anyone will succumb to temptation. Each of the four couples have their own unique challenges, ranging from insecurity and infidelity to intimacy.
Once again, host Mark L. Walberg will guide the couples through the experiment as they decide whether they want to continue their relationship or go their separate ways.
Each couple will get updates on their partners while they spend time apart and will know whether they've been faithful or not.
Below, we're sharing everything you need to know about the couples participating in Season 2.
'Temptation Island' Season 2 Couples
Kaylee and Summit
Kaylee, a 24-year-old who works in the fashion industry, and Summit, a 25-year-old fitness coach, have started to realize that they're very different people while dating each other.
"Our biggest hurdle has been navigating our differences," Kaylee, who likes structure and stability, told Netflix's Tudum. "Summit and I are like fire and rain."
“Sometimes that difference is what makes things exciting,” Summit, who is quite spontaneous and not ready to settle down, said. “Other times, it drives us completely insane.”
Their ground rules: Before arriving on Temptation Island, the couple didn't set any ground rules for how far they could go with the single tempters.
"That would kind of defeat the whole purpose," Summit said. "This experience is about truth, and sometimes the truth is messy."
Still, Kaylee said they "both know what would cross the line."
Follow them on Instagram: @kayleeenneedham and @summitwallace
Sydney and Mikey
Sydney, a 24-year-old aesthetician, and Mikey, a 24-year-old live music event host, have been together for a year and a half. Now, Sydney wants her boyfriend to show more emotional maturity.
“I want him to realize that emotionally uncomfortable conversations are needed,” she told Tudum.
Luckily, Mikey is willing to put in the effort for Sydney.
"Personally, I want to work on being more mature and romantic," he said.
Their ground rules: The couple didn't set strict rules before the experiment, but Sydney said the "unspoken" rule is "no physical touch."
"Flirting is allowed," Mikey added.
Follow them on Instagram: @jussmikey and @thesydneymorgan
Scarlett and Cole

Scarlett is a 29-year-old marketing agency owner, and Cole is a 27-year-old real estate agent, developer and entrepreneur. They've been dating for two years, but are ready to see if they're meant for each other.
"I believe that the most challenging experiences often lead to the greatest growth," Scarlett said.
Trust is an issue for the couple, especially since Cole tells little lies   due to his fear of confrontation.
Their ground rules: The couple both agreed to avoid physical touch while interacting with the singles.
Follow them on Instagram: @jussmikey and @thesydneymorgan
Shyanne and Jack

Shyanne, a 23-year-old entrepreneur, has been in a relationship with 25-year-old Jack for five years and they've known each other since high school. After dating for three years, Jack cheated on Shyanne.
"That scar never fully goes away," she told Tudum. "It does come back, and it does get brought up."
Their ground rules: The couple didn't specify ground rules to Tudum. However, Jack first suggested that the couple sign up for "Temptation Island" to help them "get past" his prior infidelity.
Follow them on Instagram: @shyanne.blankenship and @jackk.mason

title: Michael J. Fox Saw a Video Saying He'd Died. Read His Cheeky Response
summary: Michael J. Fox is reassuring fans that he's doing just fine after a CNN report was published saying he had died
story: Michael J. Fox is reassuring fans that he's doing just fine after a CNN report was published saying he had died.
The actor addressed the mishap in a post on Threads on April 8.

"How do you react when you turn on the TV and CNN is reporting your death? Do you…A) switch to MNSBC, or whatever they are calling themselves these days, (B) Pour scolding hot water on your lap, if it hurts your fine, (C) Call your wife, hopefully she’s concerned but reassuring, (D) Relax, they do this once every year, (E) Ask yourself wtf ?" Fox wrote.
The 64-year-old ended his post on a light note, writing, "I thought the world was ending, but apparently it’s just me and I’m ok. Love, Mike."
CNN accidentally published an article and video titled “Remembering the life of actor Michael J. Fox” earlier this week.
A CNN spokesperson shared the following statement with NBC News: "The package was published in error; we have removed it from our platforms and send our apologies to Michael J. Fox and his family."
Fox's fans responded to his Threads post and shared his signature sense of humor.
"Go outside and ask the first person you come across: 'Can you see me?' Glad you're ok btw!" one wrote.
Another commented, "Well I hadn’t seen it reported so I’m glad I saw this first… "
Actor Kathy Griffin also weighed in, writing, "You’re a helluva ghost. ❤️😂."

Fox, who has Parkinson’s disease, has spoken openly about his health over the years and opened up about his diagnosis in a documentary in 2023. In the documentary, the actor revealed how his wife, Tracy Pollan, reacted to the news.
"‘In sickness and in health,’ I remember her whispering," he said. "No one outside of my family knew."
Fox learned that he had Parkinson's when he was just 29 years old. However, he waited quite some time before he went public with his diagnosis.

“It was seven or eight years after I had been diagnosed ... (and) the paparazzi and stuff, they would stand outside my apartment and heckle at me, like, ‘What’s the matter with you?’” Fox previously told Entertainment Tonight.
After going public with the news, Fox was touched to see how people responded.

“It was a great surprise to me that people responded the way they responded. They responded with interest, in the desire to find an answer to the disease, and then I saw that as a great opportunity. I didn’t get put in this position to squander it,” he said.

title: Maggie Baird Is Writing a Recipe Book for Kids Billie Eilish and Finneas With ‘Things They Grew up With’
summary: Maggie Baird says she has a book of recipes for her kids, Billie Eilish and Finneas O’Connell, that she constantly updates for them.
story: Maggie Baird is still working hard to feed her kids.
The climate activist, Support+Feed founder and mother of Grammy-winning artists Billie Eilish and Finneas O’Connell says she has spent the last several years writing a recipe book for her kids.
“They have it, but I’m constantly replacing and adding,” she tells TODAY.com. “So, it’s just the recipes I’ve cooked their whole lives, or family favorites, even from my own family.”
Baird, who shared her creamy vegetable pasta recipe on TODAY, says the book is laced with anecdotes about their family, too.

“Unfortunately, my parents both died, so my children didn’t grow up with grandparents, so I’ve been including those recipes and now I’ve been adding stories about them, so they know how my mom made apple pie for my dad. She made cherry pie for my dad for years until he told her he didn’t actually like cherry pie. He liked apple.”
Baird wanted to mix passages about their lineage with the recipes she’s tinkered with over time.

“I’m trying to share stories with them, but they’re also all veganized recipes, whether they came historically through the family or they’re things they grew up with. I don’t actually cook daily with recipes. I do just look at what I have on hand and what I need to make. I realized how to make the recipe because my kids were like, ‘How do you make that?’ I’m like, ‘Well ...’”
Baird, who is working on a new lifestyle and cooking show called “Climate Kitchen,” believes it is essential to pass these recipes down.
“So I really had to write them down and, you know, go, ‘OK, if you follow this recipe exactly, I promise you it will taste good. Now, feel free to change it anytime you want,’” she adds.
Baird also says one dish in particular stands out for her kids.

“My kids both really love my sesame peanut noodles, which were inspired from living in New York because when I lived in New York in the ‘80s, one of the only takeout things you could get at the time was these cold sesame noodles,” she says.
“And then we moved to L.A., they didn’t really have those here. And they’re not what people think of, like with buckwheat and sesame; they’re very peanut-buttery. You get them in New York.
“Many, many decades ago now, I veganized that, and it’s always been a hit in our family.”

title: Amazon Discontinues Access to Kindle Store For 13 Models, Sparking Backlash
summary: Amazon has announced that it will be discontinuing support to older Kindle models, angering owners of devices they say still work fine.
story: Amazon has announced that it will be discontinuing support to older Kindle models, angering owners of devices they say still work fine.
The company sent out an email on April 7 to users stating it will no longer be supporting certain Kindle models as of May 20, 2026.
"We are discontinuing support for Kindle devices released in 2012 or earlier," the company wrote.
Here's what that means, plus the reaction from Kindle users.
Can You Still Use Your Kindle from 2012 or Earlier?
Yes, they won't just stop working. Any of the books a user has already downloaded to Kindle can still be accessed.
However, several crucial functions and services will no longer be available.
What Will No Longer Work on the Affected Kindle Models?
Amazon shared in its email to customers that the following functions will no longer be available on the affected Kindle models.

You will not be able to purchase, borrow or download additional books on the Kindles as of May 20. If you de-register or factory reset the Kindle, you will not be able to re-register it or use it in any way. You can no longer borrow books using Wi-Fi and the "Send to Kindle" option, which means popular apps like Libby for checking e-books out of your local library will no longer work.
You will not be able to purchase, borrow or download additional books on the Kindles as of May 20.
If you de-register or factory reset the Kindle, you will not be able to re-register it or use it in any way.
You can no longer borrow books using Wi-Fi and the "Send to Kindle" option, which means popular apps like Libby for checking e-books out of your local library will no longer work.
Amazon noted that any user who buys a new Kindle will still have access to their library from the older Kindle model.
What Kindle Models Are Affected?
Amazon is ending its support for the following models:

Kindle 1st Generation (2007)Kindle 2nd Generation (2009) Kindle DX (2009)Kindle DX Graphite (2010)Kindle Keyboard (2010)Kindle 4 (2011)Kindle Touch (2011)Kindle 5 (2012)Kindle Paperwhite 1st Generation (2012)Kindle Fire 1st Generation (2011)Kindle Fire 2nd Generation (2012)Kindle Fire HD 7 (2012)Kindle Fire HD 8.9 (2012)
Kindle 1st Generation (2007)
Kindle 2nd Generation (2009)

Kindle 5 (2012)
Kindle Paperwhite 1st Generation (2012)
Kindle Fire 1st Generation (2011)
Kindle Fire 2nd Generation (2012)
Kindle Fire HD 7 (2012)
Kindle Fire HD 8.9 (2012)
Why is Amazon Ending Support for Older Kindle Models?

“These models have been supported for at least 14 years — some as long as 18 years — but technology has come a long way in that time, and these devices will no longer be supported moving forward," Amazon said in a statement when contacted for comment. "We are notifying those still actively using them and offering promotions to help with the transition to newer devices. Their accounts and Kindle Library also remain fully accessible through the free Kindle app and Kindle for Web.”
What Has the Reaction Been to Amazon's Announcement?
Owners of Kindles that are at least 14 years old have shared their discontent on social media, saying the devices are still working fine.

"I’m so upset…My Kindle is fifteen years old," one author wrote on X. "It still works fine, and I want no other. I read a lot, and this is the only e-reader that doesn’t hurt my eyes. None of the new models have a screen that so perfectly mimics paper.  And now Amazon is forcing me to buy a new one. I don’t need a touchscreen or sound, and I definitely don’t want adverts!"

"Stupidly and naively, it didn’t occur to me that Amazon would force-retire my Kindle Fire 7 that my wife gave me for an anniversary well over a decade ago, one of my most treasured possessions," another Kindle owner wrote on X. "It wasn’t hurting anyone. It just sat there and worked every time I asked."

"This is A NIGHTMARE," Business Insider correspondent Katie Notopoulos wrote on X. "Amazon is bricking old Kindles, including my beloved Kindle 5 (with the side buttons). I hate the touch screen versions, I’ve tried them and I’m always accidentally flipping pages."
"This is so lame," another owner wrote on X. "I bought my kindle in 2011 and it still works perfectly fine."

title: Sharon Stone on the 1 Thing People Miss in That Infamous 'Basic Instinct' Scene
summary: Sharon Stone reveals one of the "big deals" people miss in the famous "Basic Instinct" scene and the surprising thing that required approval before filming it.
story: It's been more than three decades since Sharon Stone starred opposite Michael Douglas in the steamy thriller "Basic Instinct."
Despite being released back in 1992, her portrayal of Catherine Tramell, a crime novelist accused of brutally murdering her boyfriend, is still making headlines.
Most notably, it's the film's interrogation scene, in which Stone uncrosses her legs, offering a split-second flash of nudity, that remains gossip fodder nearly 35 years later.
It's ironic given that in the years since the incendiary moment was filmed, nudity has become increasingly commonplace in both movies and television shows.
Among them? "Euphoria," in which Stone plays Patty Lance, an executive showrunner for its third and final season.

"It's phenomenal," Stone raves of HBO's Emmy-winning teen drama. "Ashley and Sam Levinson, who made this thing and developed and produced ... they built these stars," she says of its breakout cast including Zendaya, Maude Apatow, Jacob Elordi, Sydney Sweeney and Hunter Schafer, among others. The late Eric Dane also starred and makes an appearance in Season 3 in one of his final roles.
"They found talent, talent, talent, talent, talent," Stone continues during a sit-down interview with TODAY.com. "They find nothing but talent and develop it."
Calling the the show a "ride," Stone raves that "Euphoria" is both "intense" and "unbelievable."

"It's so good. I just think it should be shown in every school in America. Adults watch it and they're like, 'Oh, that's not how it is.' You know what? I raised three boys as a single mom, by myself, and I am telling you, that's exactly what's happening in the world,"  says Stone.

It's a world that's been drastically altered by technology and social media since "Basic Instinct" was released in the early '90s and is also a markedly different one from when Stone first began acting.

"('Basic Instinct') was 30 years ago, right? You think about TV 30 years before that. We had 'Dragnet' and 'Mannix,' look at the arc from that to that," explains Stone. "And people thinking that if they might have seen something, it was the most scandalous thing in the world. Not because they did see something, but because they might have seen something."

In fact, according to Stone, when she first started in the business, female actors were required to keep their feet crossed at the ankle while on camera. Another rule? They weren't allowed to show their armpits.

"One of the big deals that people never talk about in the interrogation scene in 'Basic Instinct,' is that I had to get approval in the scene because I had bare armpits and I raised my arms over my head," she says.

"My armpits had to be approved, just like crossing my legs. My armpits. So, let's not really discount the fact that it wasn't just that 'Did we see something?' I had to get my armpits-raised-over-my-head approved."
A lot has changed since then. Most notably, women, says Stone, who've banded together to help shift a male-dominated power dynamic.

"Women are not singular. We are not isolated. We are not just talking about some man. We are not only relevant because of 'the man' in the room. We're relevant to each other. We are a sisterhood. We care more about each other than we care about the one man who thinks he runs the room," she says.
"They keep telling us, 'We're going to take away all your rights.' And we're like, 'OK, then we'll all be together as one and then we'll see how that goes.'"
Stone also weighs in on the current state of the world, saying she's got a list of questions she'd like answered.
"I want to know how we are going to live through, survive, repair, recover and love each other in these ever-changing times," she says emphatically.

"I want to understand what I can do to continue to be of service, of value, of interest and be interesting and interested during these ever-changing times. I want to understand how to let go of the thing we knew for so long that no longer exists and acknowledge our collective grieving," Stone continues before concluding.

"I want to understand how to acknowledge your grief, my grief. And also don't forget to acknowledge each other's joy and figure out how to create joy in this present moment while we release all this joy into the sea of change."

title: Megan Moroney, Miranda Lambert Lead the 2026 ACM Awards Nominees. See the Full List
summary: TODAY exclusively revealed the nominees for entertainer of the year and song of the year at ACM Awards. See the full list.
story: The Academy of County Music Awards has a sneak peek at who may soon take home some hardware.
Nominees for entertainer of the year and song of the year were announced exclusively on TODAY April 9 in advance of all of the nominees being revealed.

The entertainer of the year field may look familiar — last year’s winner, Lainey Wilson, is once again nominated. She’ll go up against a few stars who were also nominated last year, including Luke Combs, Cody Johnson, Jelly Roll, Chris Stapleton and Morgan Wallen.

Wilson is also up for song of the year for “Somewhere Over Laredo.” She'll square up in that category against fellow entertainer of the year nominees Megan Moroney (“Am I Okay?”) and Chris Stapleton (“A Song to Sing,” with Miranda Lambert).

title: Author Freida McFadden Reveals Real Identity After Using Pen Name for 13 Years
summary: For years, readers have only known bestselling author Freida McFadden by her pen name.
story: For years, readers have only known bestselling author Freida McFadden by her pen name. Now, she's sharing her real name — and explaining why she's kept her identity under wraps all this time.

“I’m at a point in my career when I’m tired of this being a secret. I’m tired of people debating if I’m a real person or if I’m three men,” McFadden told USA Today in an interview published April 8. “I am a real person and I have a real identity and I don’t have anything to hide.”
McFadden is known for her psychological thrillers, including "The Housemaid," which was adapted into a 2025 movie starring Amanda Seyfried and Sydney Sweeney.
Prior to becoming an author, McFadden worked as a doctor treating brain disorders. Because of this, she decided to write under a pen name.

“My whole goal was to keep it a secret until I was (ready to) step back from my doctor job, so it wouldn’t be like everyone I work with suddenly knew and it compromised my ability to do my job,” McFadden said.
Readers know McFadden by her pen name. In photos and her official headshot, she wears glasses and a wig. But in her everyday life, she goes by her real name, Sara Cohen.
The author said she does wear glasses in her real life and she simply wears a wig because she has "no idea how to style my hair."
“It’s so much more boring than anything that happens in my books,” she said.
These days, McFadden only works in the medical field "once or twice a month" after stepping away from full-time work as a doctor in 2023.
While she was pulling double duty working as an author and doctor, McFadden's coworkers did learn about her work as a writer and were "really nice about it."
Still, the author learned it's hard to give your all to two careers at once.
"I just realized I was completely overwhelmed from trying to do both," she said.
McFadden knows that many readers have been curious to know her real identity, but she doesn't think it will change much.

"Even though I haven’t told my real name until now, I feel like I have shared the real me all along and everything I’ve told them has been the truth," she said. "Even though the name will be a surprise, nothing else will. I’ve always been genuine with my readers."
In December 2025, McFadden sat down with Jenna Bush Hager for an “Open Book with Jenna” podcast episode and opened up about her double life as an author and physician.

During the discussion, McFadden acknowledged that some people had questioned if she uses artificial intelligence to write her books, especially since she had been able to balance two careers for so long.
“I think every author gets the ‘AI is writing her books,’ even though most of (my books) are written before AI,” McFadden said.
The author also said she'd heard a rumor saying she was really "three men."
“It’s sort of a compliment,” McFadden said. “Like it will take three men to do what I actually do.”
The author also addressed her decision to write under a pseudonym, saying, “I never for a moment considered not writing under a pen name. I just wanted to keep it separate.”

title: Hoda Kotb and Savannah Guthrie Will Reunite at TODAY Anchor Desk, Filling in for Craig Melvin on Vacation
summary: Savannah Guthrie said on TODAY April 9 that Hoda Kotb will be filling in for co-anchor Craig Melvin starting on April 13 while Craig is taking time off for spring break.
story: Reunited and it feels so good!
Savannah Guthrie announced on TODAY April 9 that Hoda Kotb will be filling in for co-anchor Craig Melvin starting on April 13 while Craig is taking time off for spring break.

“Speaking of tomorrow, Craig you are heading off on a very well-deserved vacation, you’ve been holding it down here for a long time,” Savannah said on TODAY. “And Monday, we’re going to try a new host here. Her name is Hoda.”
“Oh, I’ve heard of her,” Craig joked.
“So that will be fun,” Savannah said.

Hoda and Savannah will reunite as co-anchors for the first time since Hoda’s final day on Jan. 10, 2025, after she announced she was stepping down. The two were co-anchors together for seven years before Hoda stepped back from TODAY while remaining part of the NBC family.
They became the first female duo in history to host TODAY when Hoda was named co-anchor in 2018. In celebrating five years together in 2023, they reflected on their chemistry together.

“Can I just say that the stuff that we show on TV is fun, but the stuff I love the most is the stuff that’s not on TV,” Hoda said. “I’m just thinking about our morning and there are so many (moments) just like that, that happen before the cameras all go on, and we really get to be us.”

Ahead of their upcoming reunion, Hoda had been filling in for Savannah as TODAY co-anchor for nearly two months while Savannah was with her family following her mother’s disappearance from her Arizona home on Feb. 1. A search for Nancy Guthrie, 84, and an investigation by local authorities and the FBI remains ongoing.
Savannah returned on April 6 to rejoin Craig in anchoring the show. She said in her first show back that “it is good to be home” and also drew a loud cheer from TODAY fans on Rockefeller Plaza.
She appeared in front of the crowd while holding hands with Jenna Bush Hager as they joined Craig, Sheinelle Jones, Al Roker and Carson Daly.
“You guys have been so beautiful,” she told the crowd. “I’ve received so many letters, so much kindness to me and my whole family. We feel it. We feel your prayers, so thank you so much.”

title: Keith Morrison Addresses ‘Ketamine Queen’ At Her Sentencing For Role in Stepson Matthew Perry’s Death
summary: Keith Morrison, the stepfather of the late Matthew Perry, called the late "Friends" actor a "brilliant talented man" before telling the woman who illegally sold him the ketamine that killed him, "I feel bad for you."
story: Keith Morrison, the stepfather of the late Matthew Perry, called the late "Friends" actor a "brilliant talented man" before telling the woman who illegally sold him the ketamine that killed him, "I feel bad for you."

On April 8, Jasveen Sangha, known as the “Ketamine Queen” to her drug customers, was sentenced to 15 years behind bars and three years’ supervised release for her connection to Perry’s 2023 overdose death, NBC News reported. Sangha had previously pleaded guilty to illegally selling the hallucinogenic anesthetic in September 2025.
“I feel bad for you. I don’t hate you, I’m not angry at you. You’re a drug dealer,” Morrison told Sangha in the courtroom. “The fact is you supplied an addict."

Morrison, who has been married to the late actor’s mom, Suzanne Perry, since 1981, called Perry's addiction “the big terrible thing,” seemingly referencing Perry's 2022 memoir of the same name, noting now it is a “daily, grinding sadness and sorrow we all feel.”
While Morrison lamented the death of Perry, he also recalled his gifts.
“There was a spark to that man that I’ve never seen anywhere else ... he was one of a kind,” he said.
“He should have had another act," Morrison added. "Two more acts.”
Perry’s stepmother, Debbie Perry, who married John Bennett Perry in 1981, also addressed Sangha in a victim impact statement submitted to the court.
“The pain you caused to hundreds, maybe thousands is irreversible. There is no joy, no light,” she said.
Sangha said she took “full responsibility” for what happened.
“My poor choices, addiction ... led me here,” she added.

After the sentencing, Morrison shared a brief statement outside of the courthouse. The “Dateline” correspondent said, “We miss Matthew dreadfully, of course, and I feel bad for the family of the perpetrator here as well. Nobody won today.”
He also said the “very fair” judge chose a “highly reasoned sentence.”

When asked if he thought Sangha took to heart the words he shared in court, Morrison replied, “Yes, I do. I looked down and she was visibly emotional about it. I mean, look, you’d have to have a heart of stone to wake up every morning and make a business out of feeding off the addictions of vulnerable people who are desperate for drugs. Then when you’re forced to confront what you have done, if you don’t feel some sense of shame or sorrow, then you’re not even human.”
He continued, “And she is clearly human. She is now facing her sentence, and I think she’ll do fine in prison.”
Perry was found face down and unresponsive in the pool at his Pacific Palisades home on Oct. 28, 2023. The 54-year-old actor was pronounced dead at the scene.

Morrison, his wife and three of Perry’s sisters — Caitlin, Emily and Madeline Morrison — previously spoke about grieving the loss of their son and brother on the first anniversary of his death in an interview with TODAY. The October 2024 sit-down came after charges were first announced for the five people connected to Perry’s death.

“What I’m hoping, and I think the agencies that got involved in this are hoping, that people who have put themselves in the business of supplying people with the drugs that’ll kill them — they are now on notice. It doesn’t matter what your professional credentials are. You’re goin’ down, baby,” Keith Morrison said at the time.

Speaking about his stepson’s impact, Morrison added, “What he taught the world is that no amount of money will cure an addict. It needs something else. That’s what we’re trying to do (with the foundation),” referring to the Matthew Perry Foundation of Canada that the family founded that same month.
Prior to her sentencing, Sangha pleaded guilty in September 2025 to five federal charges involving Perry’s death. She was arrested in August 2024 and has been in federal custody since then.
In the courtroom, Sangha reflected on the “horrible decisions that ultimately proved tragic."
“I pray for forgiveness every day. Thank you for giving me the harshest reality check of my life. Thank you for taking me out of the equation," she said.

After the sentencing, her attorney, Mark Geragos, said about the decision, “I’m bitterly disappointed. If you weren’t in the courtroom, the argument is there’s no way that Jasveen is five times more culpable than the person who injected Matthew Perry with the drug or the doctor who got the drug.”

“To me, it’s absurd in the literal meaning of the word, and if this is what the criminal justice system, or the theory of crime and punishment is, that the dealer should get five times as much as the person who injects the drug, who is there to take care of somebody, then maybe I’ve been practicing too long,” he added.

According to the U.S. attorneys involved in the case, Sangha said in her plea agreement that she had worked with a man named Erik Fleming to supply Perry with 51 vials of ketamine. The drug was then given to Perry by his live-in personal assistant named Kenneth Iwamasa, prosecutors said.

Fleming and Iwamasa are two of the five people who were charged in the case. Fleming pleaded guilty in August 2024 to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine and one count of distribution of ketamine resulting in death, NBC News reported, and Iwamasa also pleaded guilty the same month to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine causing death.
They will receive their sentences later this month.

Additionally, Dr. Mark Chavez pleaded guilty to one count of conspiracy to distribute ketamine in August 2024. Dr. Salvador Plasencia pleaded not guilty to the same crime. In December 2025, Chavez was sentenced to three years of supervised release, including eight months of home confinement. Plasencia was given two and a half years in prison.

title: Where Do Kristin Cavallari and Lauren Conrad Stand After Their 'Laguna Beach' Feud? They Told Us
summary: Kristin Cavallari and Lauren Conrad feuded for years. Where does their relationship stand now?
story: Kristin Cavallari and Lauren Conrad have come a long way from their "Laguna Beach" days.
When the MTV reality series debuted in 2004, viewers were introduced to a group of high school students from Laguna Beach.

The friend group encountered, and created, plenty of drama over the course of the show's three seasons. Cavallari and Conrad were at the center of the drama and were involved in an alleged love triangle with the show’s resident hunk, Stephen Colletti.
Cavallari and Conrad feuded for years, but they recently got back together for a "Laguna Beach" reunion nearly 20 years after “Laguna Beach” aired its last episode.
Ahead of the reunion's premiere on the Roku Channel on April 10, Conrad and Cavallari spoke to TODAY.com about their relationship, then and now.
What Happened Between Kristin Cavallari and Lauren Conrad?
One of the main storylines of "Laguna Beach" followed a love triangle between Colletti, Conrad and Cavallari.

Colletti and Conrad knew each other as children and continued their friendship through high school. Meanwhile, Colletti and Cavallari were high school sweethearts who were involved in on-again, off-again relationship.
Throughout the series, Conrad and Cavallari exhibited animosity towards each other.
In 2022, Conrad appeared on Colletti and Cavallari's podcast,  “Back to the Beach with Kristin and Stephen.” During the episode, the two addressed their feud.

“My take on it is, you and I really never had any beef, really” Cavallari told Conrad. “Obviously, there was a little truth to what happened with the three of us. I felt like MTV coming kept it alive and made it way worse than it ever would have been. Is that also your take? And you can be brutally honest about it.”
“Yeah, I think it was done when they started filming,” Conrad said, adding that she and Cavallari had a fight before filming started but later moved on.
“We got into it and then made up,” Conrad added. “I mean, I don’t think we were best friends. But we were like, ‘It’s fine.’”

Cavallari suggested that she and Conrad “never had an issue” with one another, but Conrad disagreed, saying, “I mean, we had an issue with each other. But it had been squashed. They ran with it. It made for an interesting show.”

In a trailer for the "Laguna Beach" reunion, Cavallari addressed the love triangle, saying, "It was the history with the two of them and Stephen being my boyfriend. I mean, just naturally, anyone probably wouldn't like the other girl in that situation."
Conrad added to the conversation, saying, "We did not like each other."
What Did Lauren Conrad and Kristin Cavallari Say About Their Relationship Now?
Nearly 20 years have passed since "Laguna Beach" ended. When asked where their feud stands today, Cavallari and Conrad shared the following update with TODAY.com.

"We did see each other a bit when we were in LA and always got along. And then even in Tennessee, I've been here, so we haven't actually hung out in a really long time. But this has been a blast. We've had so much fun doing this," Conrad said, referring to seeing Cavallari in Nashville, where she lives.
Cavallari said she appreciated the opportunity to clear the air when Conrad appeared on the podcast she hosted with Colletti in 2022.
"Obviously, it's been so long. I mean, I'm just happy that we've gotten this time together where we can truly just put everything in the past. It's been really nice," Cavallari said.
Now that both Cavallari and Conrad are parents, they can't help but watch "Laguna Beach" with a different lens.

"It's hard being a kid. There's drama with friends. There's boy-girl drama. Everything we went through was very relatable, very real. And I'm starting to see little trickles of it now as my kids get a little bit older," Cavallari said.

title: How 2 Rom-Com ‘Non-Negotiables’ Led to ‘You, Me & Tuscany’
summary: "You, Me & Tuscany" producer Will Packer and director Kat Coiro share how they brought the movie to life.
story: When bringing “You, Me & Tuscany” to life, producer Will Packer and director Kat Coiro set out to create a film centered on authenticity and a communal experience.

The Italy-set romantic comedy, in theaters April 10, stars Halle Bailey as Anna, a down-on-her luck young woman who gave up her dream of becoming a chef. Now, Anna is struggling to find her place in the world. After meeting Matteo (Lorenzo de Moor), a handsome Italian man who owns a villa that is currently unoccupied in Tuscany, she books a ticket for an unexpected adventure.

In Tuscany, she meets Matteo’s handsome cousin Michael, portrayed by Regé-Jean Page, and their chemistry is undeniable. Anna, however, finds herself in a web of lies after Matteo’s mom believes she is her son’s fiancée.
Packer, known for producing films like “Girls Trip,” “Think Like a Man” and “Ride Along,” knew the exact story he wanted to tell and what his non-negotiables were when crafting “You, Me & Tuscany.”

“It needed to be fun. It needed to be laugh-out-loud. It needed to be something that had a communal viewing experience,” Packer tells TODAY.com during a press junket at Elan Vineyards in Napa, California. “For me, that’s what was important.”

That communal experience needed to be on the big screen. With more rom-coms making streaming debuts, like “Bridget Jones: Mad About the Boy,” “People We Meet on Vacation” and “The Idea of You,” Packer believes “now more than ever,” audiences need a reason to go to the theaters. “Put the phone down. Turn off the news. Turn off what’s happening in real life,” he says. “And have an escapist experience.”
To ensure that, Packer says he and Coiro made the film “with the audience in mind.”

“We wanted people to go to the movie and sit next to people because it really elevates your viewing experience,” he shares. “I love the fact that it’s going to theaters. People have got a lot of choices, a lot of things to do, but there’s nothing out there quite like this movie, and I think this is going to cut through.”
For Coiro, her non-negotiable when directing “You, Me & Tuscany” was to film on location in Italy, giving the audience the best viewing experience.
